Class TxDependencyRegistry

java.lang.Object
ca.yorku.cmg.cnsim.engine.transaction.TxDependencyRegistry
public final class TxDependencyRegistry extends Object
Tracks transaction dependencies for simulation purposes.

The TxDependencyRegistry maintains dependencies between transactions, where transaction j can depend on one or more earlier transactions i (where i < j). Dependencies are stored using BitSet arrays for compact representation and efficient dependency checking operations. Transaction IDs are expected to be in the range [1, size], where size is specified at construction time.

The registry provides methods for adding individual dependencies, setting all dependencies for a transaction at once, checking whether all dependencies of a transaction are satisfied, and converting collections of satisfied transactions to BitSets for efficient set operations.

Since:
0.1
Author:
Sotirios Liaskos for the Conceptual Modeling Group @ York University
See Also:

  • Transaction

  • Constructor Details

    • TxDependencyRegistry

      public TxDependencyRegistry(long size)
      Creates a transaction dependency registry with the specified size.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires 0 < size && size < Integer.MAX_VALUE;
   //@ ensures this.size == (int) size;
   //@ ensures this.deps.length == this.size + 1;
      Parameters:
      size - the max number of transactions this registry will track
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if size >= Integer.MAX_VALUE || size <= 0

  • Method Details

    • addDependency

      public void addDependency(int j, int i)
      Adds a dependency indicating that transaction j depends on transaction i.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires i < j;
   //@ requires j >= 1 && j < deps.length;
   //@ requires i >= 1;
   //@ ensures deps[j].get(i) == true;
      Parameters:
      j - the transaction ID that has the dependency
      i - the transaction ID that j depends on
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if j < 1 || j > size
      IllegalArgumentException - if i < 1 || i >= j

    • addDependencies

      public void addDependencies(int id, BitSet dependencies)
      Sets all dependencies for a transaction by replacing its dependency BitSet.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires id >= 1 && id < deps.length;
   //@ ensures deps[id] == dependencies;
      Parameters:
      id - the transaction ID
      dependencies - the BitSet representing all dependencies for this transaction
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if id < 1 or id >= deps.length

    • toBitSet

      public BitSet toBitSet(Collection<Integer> satisfied)
      Converts a collection of satisfied transaction IDs into a BitSet.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires satisfied != null;
   //@ ensures \result != null;
   //@ ensures (\forall int x; satisfied.contains(x); \result.get(x));
      Parameters:
      satisfied - a collection of satisfied transaction IDs
      Returns:
      a BitSet with bits set for each ID in satisfied
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if satisfied is null

    • toBitSet

      public BitSet toBitSet(List<Transaction> satisfied)
      Converts a list of satisfied Transaction objects into a BitSet.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires satisfied != null;
   //@ ensures \result != null;
   //@ ensures (\forall Transaction tx; satisfied.contains(tx); \result.get((int) tx.getID()));
      Parameters:
      satisfied - a list of satisfied Transaction objects
      Returns:
      a BitSet with bits set for each transaction ID in satisfied
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if satisfied is null or contains null elements

    • dependenciesMet

      public boolean dependenciesMet(int j, BitSet satisfiedBits)
      Checks whether all dependencies of transaction j are satisfied (contained in satisfiedBits).

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires j >= 1 && j < deps.length;
   //@ requires satisfiedBits != null;
   //@ ensures \result == (\forall int i; deps[j].get(i); satisfiedBits.get(i));
      Parameters:
      j - the transaction ID to check
      satisfiedBits - a BitSet representing all satisfied transaction IDs
      Returns:
      true if all dependencies of j are satisfied; false otherwise
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if satisfiedBits is null
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if j < 1 or j >= deps.length

    • dependenciesMetFast

      public boolean dependenciesMetFast(int j, BitSet satisfiedBits, BitSet scratch)
      Checks whether all dependencies of transaction j are satisfied, using a reusable scratch BitSet.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires j >= 1 && j < deps.length;
   //@ requires satisfiedBits != null;
   //@ requires scratch != null;
   //@ ensures \result == (deps[j] == null || (\forall int i; deps[j].get(i); satisfiedBits.get(i)));
      Parameters:
      j - the transaction ID to check
      satisfiedBits - a BitSet representing all satisfied transaction IDs
      scratch - a reusable BitSet for temporary calculations (will be modified)
      Returns:
      true if all dependencies of j are satisfied or if deps[j] is null; false otherwise
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if satisfiedBits is null
      NullPointerException - if scratch is null
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if j < 1 or j >= deps.length

    • toString

      public String toString(int txID)
      Returns a string representation of the dependencies of the given transaction.
      Parameters:
      txID - the transaction ID whose dependencies should be printed
      Returns:
      "-1" if there are no dependencies; otherwise a semicolon-separated set like "{1;2}"
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if txID < 1 or txID >= deps.length
